introSection: Ednör - L'Attaque is an inverted roller coaster located at La Ronde in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. It is a Suspended Looping Coaster installation from Vekoma.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On 18 November 1998, Six Flags AstroWorld announced that Serial Thriller would open for the 1999 season at a cost of $10 million as part of a $15 million investment package for the park.

In January 1999, red track and teal supports for Serial Thriller were delivered to the park and arrived in a old car dealership parking lot next to the park. Excalibur was removed and subsequently dismantled from the park at the end of the 1998 season by February 1999 to make room for the new coaster.

On 28 May 1999, Serial Thriller opened to the public following a media day two days before.

During its debut season, its transfer track was modified after trains were dropped off. A new bumper was also constructed.

In 2005, Serial Thriller was repainted. Later that year, Six Flags AstroWorld closed permanently on 30 October. After the park's closure, Six Flags would relocate many of the park's rides to other Six Flags properties, while others were put up for auction or demolished for scrap. Serial Thriller was subsequently dismantled and was moved to The Great Escape & Splashwater Kingdom along with other AstroWorld ride Tidal Wave, where it sat in storage from December 2005 until July 2009.

It was subsequently shipped to La Ronde to be rebuilt there for the 2010 season. The red track was resprayed and the supports were repainted from teal to yellow. "Ednör" is a play on words of the second word of La Ronde. "Ednör" is "Ronde" spelled backwards. Ednör - L'Attaque opened on 15 May 2010.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Ednör - L'Attaque features the standard Suspended Looping Coaster design, consisting of five inversions.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Ednör - L'Attaque has a red track and yellow supports. While at Six Flags AstroWorld as Serial Thriller, the ride had red track and teal supports.

sections.title: External links
sections.content: * Serial Thriller at Six Flags AstroWorld

fullText:
Ednör - L'Attaque is an inverted roller coaster located at La Ronde in Montreal, Quebec, Canada. It is a Suspended Looping Coaster installation from Vekoma.

On 18 November 1998, Six Flags AstroWorld announced that Serial Thriller would open for the 1999 season at a cost of $10 million as part of a $15 million investment package for the park.

In January 1999, red track and teal supports for Serial Thriller were delivered to the park and arrived in a old car dealership parking lot next to the park. Excalibur was removed and subsequently dismantled from the park at the end of the 1998 season by February 1999 to make room for the new coaster.

On 28 May 1999, Serial Thriller opened to the public following a media day two days before.

During its debut season, its transfer track was modified after trains were dropped off. A new bumper was also constructed.

In 2005, Serial Thriller was repainted. Later that year, Six Flags AstroWorld closed permanently on 30 October. After the park's closure, Six Flags would relocate many of the park's rides to other Six Flags properties, while others were put up for auction or demolished for scrap. Serial Thriller was subsequently dismantled and was moved to The Great Escape & Splashwater Kingdom along with other AstroWorld ride Tidal Wave, where it sat in storage from December 2005 until July 2009.

It was subsequently shipped to La Ronde to be rebuilt there for the 2010 season. The red track was resprayed and the supports were repainted from teal to yellow. "Ednör" is a play on words of the second word of La Ronde. "Ednör" is "Ronde" spelled backwards. Ednör - L'Attaque opened on 15 May 2010.

Ednör - L'Attaque features the standard Suspended Looping Coaster design, consisting of five inversions.

Ednör - L'Attaque has a red track and yellow supports. While at Six Flags AstroWorld as Serial Thriller, the ride had red track and teal supports.
